description:
HelixArchive is a Rust-based DNA data storage engine for the Stardance NASA challenge.
Core files: src/lib.rs (encoder/decoder/GC-constraint), src/main.rs (CLI).

Work through these tasks in order — one focused change per session:
1.  Add homopolymer run detector — flag consecutive identical bases > 4 long
2.  Add FASTA format exporter — write encoded strands as standard .fasta files
3.  Add Criterion benchmarks in benches/throughput.rs for encode/decode speed
4.  Add GC distribution histogram to EncodingStats (bucket counts per 5% band)
5.  Add Reed-Solomon style per-block checksum for error detection
6.  Add DNA strand reverse-complement function
7.  Add melting temperature (Tm) estimator per strand block using nearest-neighbor model
8.  Write integration tests in tests/integration.rs covering all edge cases
9.  Add CLI 'analyze' command: reports GC%, homopolymer runs, Tm on any .strand file
10. Write a professional README.md documenting architecture and biological constraints
11. Add a Python binding skeleton using PyO3
12. Add strand visualization: print a colored ASCII map of base distribution
13. Add support for encoding images (BMP) to DNA strands
14. Add a web API skeleton using Axum for remote encoding requests
15. Add compression preprocessing (LZ4) before DNA encoding to improve GC balance

Rules:
- Make ONE small, focused, human-scaled change per session.
- Never break existing tests or function signatures.
- Add doc comments (///) to every new public function.
- Prefer extending existing files over creating unnecessary new ones.
- Keep changes under ~80 lines of new code per session.
